subject: Mathematics
title: Strong vs. Weak Arguments
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Strong vs. Weak Arguments.

hook.text: Not all arguments are equal. Some are airtight; others crumble under examination.

buildup.text: A strong argument makes its conclusion very likely given the premises. A weak one barely supports it.

discovery.text: Deductive arguments are either valid or invalid—no middle ground. Inductive ones exist on a spectrum.

twist.text: A valid deductive argument with false premises can lead to any conclusion—garbage in, garbage out.

climax.text: Critical thinking means evaluating both the logic (structure) and the truth (content) of every argument.

punchline.text: Good logic with bad facts still leads nowhere.

quiz.question: What makes an inductive argument strong?
options: The premises make the conclusion very likely
